title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, accompanied by bassist John Clayton.
The 85-minute concert featured songs like 'Almost Like Being In Love' and 'Come Dance With Me'.
Krall's performance highlighted her dual talents as a singer and pianist with strong interplay alongside Clayton.

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez in Chelsea.
Incident occurred on March 19 near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue; police found multiple stabbing victims at the scene.
Castellanos Cerrato has pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; due in court on April 21.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro area's population growth in 2025 was the slowest year since 2021, with an increase of only 11,991.
Massachusetts statewide population grew by only 15,524 in 2025, marking a continued decrease from post-pandemic trends.
Middlesex and Worcester counties saw reduced growth rates, with Suffolk, Barnstable, and Hampshire experiencing slight population declines.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod research group launched a GoFundMe to support North Atlantic right whale conservation, aiming to raise $15,000.
The Marineland Right Whale Project raised $630 so far to aid fieldwork and purchase equipment like drones and cameras.
North Atlantic right whales face extinction risks mainly from vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements, with few hundred remaining.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss was acquired from the Raiders in 2007, setting a record with 23 touchdowns in his first Patriots season.
Wes Welker was traded from the Dolphins in 2007 and became a key slot receiver, though he won no Super Bowl with the Patriots.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, performed well initially but faced suspensions, impairing his long-term impact.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected for arguing a strike call against Trevor Story during the Red Sox's 6-5 loss to the Reds.
Cora disputed C.B. Bucknor's decision not to defer to the first base umpire on Story's check swing call.
Roman Anthony's failed ABS challenge left the Red Sox without the ability to contest pitches later in the game.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story was upset with umpire C.B. Bucknor's check swing strike three call, which led to Alex Cora's ejection.
The controversial call occurred with the Red Sox down 5-4 and two runners on base late in the game.
Despite Story's strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game before losing 6-5 in the 11th inning against the opponent.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games since the Bruins returned from the Olympic break.
Zacha missed the 2026 Olympics due to a concussion, motivating his strong performance for the Boston Bruins.
Zacha, 28, has set a career-high in goals with 26 this season, contributing significantly to the Bruins' playoff push.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray lasted four innings, allowing four runs in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds.
Gray criticized his performance, stating he didn't give Boston a good chance to win the game.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray's improvement during the game, despite Boston's 6-5 loss in 11 innings.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands attended the third 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common, part of a nationwide movement.
The rally featured speeches from state leaders and performances, including the Dropkick Murphys and Good Trouble Brass Band.
Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren addressed issues like elections, military involvement, and immigration.
